name: Home SOC Lab - Splunk Detection Coverage
description: MITRE ATT&CK detection coverage for a Splunk-based home SOC lab. Green (score 100) = detection built, tested, and validated against Atomic Red Team. Yellow (score 50) = planned next-priority detection. Grey = no coverage. Built by Israel Ehiabhi Edeaghe.

techniques.comment: Detection 1: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command. Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1059.001-15. Sysmon EventID 1 command-line regex match on -enc/-encodedcommand flags. Severity: Medium.

Detection 2: LSASS Memory Dump via comsvcs.dll. Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1003.001-2. Sysmon EventID 1 command-line match on rundll32+comsvcs+MiniDump+lsass. Living-off-the-land Mimikatz equivalent. Severity: High.

Detection 3: Lateral Movement via SMB Admin Shares. Validated via manual two-VM test. Security Event 5140. Required enabling Advanced Audit Policy File Share subcategory and LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy fix. Severity: High.

Detection 4: Registry Run Key Persistence. Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1547.001-1. Sysmon EventID 13 (Registry Value Set). Tuned against real Microsoft Edge false positive observed in lab. Severity: Medium.

Detection 5: Scheduled Task Creation. Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1053.005-2. Security Event 4698. Required enabling Advanced Audit Policy Other Object Access Events - not logged by default on fresh Windows install. Severity: Medium.

Detection 6: Process Injection via anomalous process lineage (RWX DLL injection). Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1055-5. Note: classic CreateRemoteThread (T1055.003) was blocked by Windows 11 process mitigations even with full admin and Defender disabled - a genuine OS-level finding documented in Investigation 4. Severity: Medium.

Detection 7: Download Cradle / Fileless Privilege Escalation. Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1134.001-2 (Empire BC-SECURITY Get-System.ps1). IEX+IWR pattern detection. Generalized to catch any fileless PowerShell download cradle, not just this specific Empire module. Severity: High.

Detection 8: DNS Exfiltration via DNSExfiltrator using DNS-over-HTTPS. Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1048-3. Command-line signature detection on tool invocation. Exfil failed at network layer (no real C2 domain) but detection fires correctly on attempt - the security-relevant signal. Severity: High.

Detection 9: LOLBin Abuse via Rundll32. Validated vs Atomic Red Team T1218.011-9 (pcwutl.dll LaunchApplication). Generalized to cover multiple known LOLBin DLLs (pcwutl, syssetup, advpack, setupapi, ieadvpack) in one query. Severity: Medium.

PLANNED: Detect initial access via malicious email attachment. High real-world frequency. Would detect via Sysmon process creation from email client parent processes (Outlook spawning PowerShell/cmd). No new infrastructure needed - same Sysmon EventID 1 telemetry already flowing.

PLANNED: Detect attacker use of local account credentials. Directly observed in Investigation 2 (lateral movement using socadmin local account across two VMs). Detect via Security Event 4624 logon Type 3 from unexpected source addresses. Builds on existing Security log pipeline.

PLANNED: Password brute force detection via Security Event 4625 (failed logon) spike analysis. High-value, low-complexity SPL correlation. Builds directly on existing Security log pipeline - no new telemetry needed.

PLANNED: Command obfuscation detection beyond encoded commands. Extends Detection 1 (T1059.001 encoded PowerShell) to cover string concatenation, character substitution, and other obfuscation patterns. Same Sysmon EventID 1 telemetry, broader regex patterns needed.

PLANNED: Detect attempts to disable security tools (Defender, Sysmon, audit logging). Security Event 4719 (audit policy changed) + Sysmon service stop events. Critical gap - an attacker who disables Sysmon defeats the entire lab telemetry pipeline.

PLANNED: Detect Windows Event Log clearing via Security Event 1102 (Security log cleared) and System Event 104 (any log cleared). Simple, high-value detection - log clearing is almost always malicious. Directly relevant since an attacker clearing logs defeats the Splunk pipeline.

PLANNED: SAM database credential dumping. Natural extension of Detection 2 (LSASS dumping). Detectable via Sysmon registry access to HKLM\SAM. Same credential-access tactic, different technique - coverage of both strengthens the tactic-level posture.

PLANNED: Detect tool download to endpoint (certutil, bitsadmin, PowerShell IWR/WebClient downloading executables). Extends existing Detection 7 download cradle logic to cover non-IEX delivery methods. Same Sysmon EventID 1 telemetry, different command-line patterns.
